issues.body:
The round-1 blocker is only partially fixed. build:wasm, build:wasm:lean, build:reason-wasm, build:rsp-wasm, build:text-wasm now pass `--no-opt` to wasm-pack and chain `_optimize:wasm` (js/scripts/optimize-wasm.mjs), which does `process.exit(1)` when wasm-opt is not on PATH (spawnSync ENOENT). This PR installs binaryen in gui.yml, pages.yml, publish.yml, and site-visual.yml — but the SAME modified scripts are also run by lanes that received NO binaryen step:

- js.yml:116 — `npm run build` (working-directory js/) = `npm run build:wasm && npm run build:ts`. This is a REQUIRED per-PR gate (the `js` check). `grep -nE 'binaryen|wasm-opt' .github/workflows/js.yml` = no match. With this change the `js` gate fails at `_optimize:wasm`; this PR's own `js` check should be red.
- release.yml:347 — `- run: npm run build:wasm` (working-directory js/) in the GUI/Tauri release job. No binaryen -> the release build fails.
- site-e2e-hero.yml:97 — `cd ../js && npm run build:wasm:lean`. No binaryen.
- nightly-full-sweep.yml:184/294/386/495 — build:wasm / build:wasm:lean. No binaryen.

(The publish.yml npm-eyereasoner-compat and npm-solid-server jobs build packages/eyereasoner-compat and packages/solid-server, whose scripts still call wasm-pack WITHOUT --no-opt, so they rely on the bundled optimizer and correctly need no binaryen — those are fine.)
issues.fix_hint: Add the same `sudo apt-get update -q && sudo apt-get install -y binaryen` step (before the wasm build) to every remaining lane that runs the modified js build scripts: js.yml (the build job), release.yml (before line 347's Build wasm bundle), site-e2e-hero.yml (before line 97), and nightly-full-sweep.yml (each job that runs build:wasm/build:wasm:lean). To avoid this recurring, grep ALL of .github/workflows for `build:wasm`, `build:wasm:lean`, `build:reason-wasm`, `build:rsp-wasm`, `build:text-wasm`, and bare `npm run build` and confirm each such job installs binaryen. Alternatively, make optimize-wasm.mjs degrade gracefully when wasm-opt is absent — but that silently ships an un-optimized bundle differing from the published one, so installing binaryen everywhere is preferred.
issues.title: build:wasm --no-opt hard-requires system wasm-opt, but js.yml / release.yml / site-e2e-hero.yml / nightly-full-sweep.yml still don't install Binaryen

issues.body: The README now states the main and lean engine bundles use `-O3` 'where query execution is the priority', while the lazy reason/RSP/text bundles use size-first `-Oz`. -O3 typically produces a larger _bg.wasm than the prior wasm-pack default `-O` / the size-leaning crate metadata, raising npm download size and browser compile time for the EAGERLY-loaded main bundle. No measured size delta is given, and the HARD-gated wasm_bundle_bytes ratchet runs on the raw PRE-opt artifact (README itself notes 'the raw wasm_bundle_bytes Cargo-artifact ratchet remains unchanged'), so a shipped-size regression on the -O3 bundle is not caught by any gate — only the TREND-ONLY wasm_opt_bundle_bytes series in ci-bench.sh reflects it.
issues.fix_hint: Record the measured shipped-size delta (main and lean, before/after -O3) in the PR body and confirm the increase is acceptable for the eagerly-loaded bundle. Consider whether a soft budget on the post-opt shipped size is warranted rather than trend-only visibility.
issues.title: -O3 on the eagerly-loaded main/lean bundle increases download/compile size vs the prior size-leaning default; tradeoff asserted but unquantified

summary:
Round 2 addresses the round-1 blocker in the four lanes it named: gui.yml (5 jobs), pages.yml, publish.yml (the main `npm` job), and site-visual.yml all now `apt-get install -y binaryen` before running the js build scripts. optimize-wasm.mjs is well-formed (level allowlist, atomic .optimized+renameSync, cleans partial output, clear ENOENT message). The two sibling publish jobs (npm-eyereasoner-compat, npm-solid-server) build DIFFERENT packages whose scripts still use wasm-pack's bundled optimizer (no --no-opt), so they correctly need no binaryen. The ci-bench.sh -Oz->-O3 trend-metric change stays guarded by `command -v wasm-opt` and is internally consistent.

BLOCKER — the fix is INCOMPLETE. The same root cause (js/package.json build:wasm{,:lean}/reason/rsp/text now pass --no-opt and chain optimize-wasm.mjs, which process.exit(1) on wasm-opt ENOENT) still breaks lanes NOT covered by this PR and NOT given a binaryen step:
- js.yml:116 — `npm run build` (=build:wasm) in the REQUIRED per-PR `js` gate. No binaryen -> this very PR's `js` check should be red.
- release.yml:347 — `npm run build:wasm` in the release/Tauri-shell path. No binaryen -> release build fails.
- site-e2e-hero.yml:97 — `npm run build:wasm:lean`. No binaryen.
- nightly-full-sweep.yml:184/294/386/495 — build:wasm / build:wasm:lean. No binaryen.
None of grep binaryen|wasm-opt matches in those four files.

MINOR (persists from round 1): js/README.md asserts -O3 on the eagerly-loaded main/lean bundle 'where query execution is the priority' but gives no shipped-size delta; the HARD wasm_bundle_bytes ratchet is pre-opt so a shipped-size regression is invisible.

Progress: stagnant — the headline blocker remains at blocker severity (build hard-requires system wasm-opt; several CI lanes still don't install it); four lanes fixed, four equivalent lanes missed. The shacl-wasm nit is gone.
